# Browser Playground {#sec-playground}

The playground keeps the manual page open while compilation and execution happen in a local Docker container. The first example is deliberately small: edit a `Hello IPPL` program, compile it, run it with MPI, inspect stdout, and edit again.

## Start the local runner

Start the runner on the local machine:

```bash
docker run --rm \
  -p 127.0.0.1:5050:5050 \
  ghcr.io/ippl-framework/ippl-runner:latest
```

The runner prints a token:

```text
IPPL runner token: ...
```

Paste that token into the field below. The token prevents unrelated websites from silently sending compile jobs to the local runner.

## Hello IPPL

```{=html}
<style>
.ippl-playground {
  border: 1px solid #d7dee8;
  border-radius: 8px;
  overflow: hidden;
  margin: 1.25rem 0;
  background: #f8fafc;
}
.ippl-playground-toolbar {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 0.75rem;
  align-items: end;
  padding: 0.9rem;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #d7dee8;
  background: #eef3f8;
}
.ippl-playground-field {
  display: grid;
  gap: 0.25rem;
}
.ippl-playground-field label {
  font-size: 0.78rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: #334155;
}
.ippl-playground input {
  min-height: 2.1rem;
  border: 1px solid #bac6d3;
  border-radius: 6px;
  padding: 0.35rem 0.5rem;
  background: white;
}
.ippl-playground button {
  min-height: 2.1rem;
  border: 1px solid #1d4ed8;
  border-radius: 6px;
  padding: 0.35rem 0.8rem;
  color: white;
  background: #1d4ed8;
}
.ippl-playground button.secondary {
  color: #1d4ed8;
  background: white;
}
.ippl-playground-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1.15fr) minmax(0, 0.85fr);
  min-height: 34rem;
}
.ippl-playground textarea,
.ippl-playground pre {
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 34rem;
  margin: 0;
  border: 0;
  border-radius: 0;
  padding: 1rem;
  font: 0.88rem/1.45 ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.ippl-playground textarea {
  resize: vertical;
  border-right: 1px solid #d7dee8;
  background: #ffffff;
}
.ippl-playground pre {
  overflow: auto;
  color: #d8e7ff;
  background: #101828;
  white-space: pre-wrap;
}
.ippl-playground-status {
  padding: 0.65rem 0.9rem;
  border-top: 1px solid #d7dee8;
  color: #334155;
  background: white;
}
@media (max-width: 900px) {
  .ippl-playground-grid {
    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  }
  .ippl-playground textarea {
    border-right: 0;
    border-bottom: 1px solid #d7dee8;
  }
}
</style>

<div class="ippl-playground" id="ippl-playground">
  <div class="ippl-playground-toolbar">
    <div class="ippl-playground-field" style="min-width: 18rem;">
      <label for="ippl-runner-token">Runner token</label>
      <input id="ippl-runner-token" type="password" autocomplete="off" placeholder="Paste token printed by Docker">
    </div>
    <div class="ippl-playground-field" style="width: 7rem;">
      <label for="ippl-runner-ranks">MPI ranks</label>
      <input id="ippl-runner-ranks" type="number" min="1" max="4" value="2">
    </div>
    <button class="secondary" id="ippl-check-runner" type="button">Check runner</button>
    <button id="ippl-run-code" type="button">Compile and run</button>
  </div>
  <div class="ippl-playground-grid">
    <textarea id="ippl-source" spellcheck="false">#include "Ippl.h"

int main(int argc, char* argv[]) {
    ippl::initialize(argc, argv);

    *ippl::Info &lt;&lt; "Hello from rank "
                &lt;&lt; ippl::Comm-&gt;rank()
                &lt;&lt; " of "
                &lt;&lt; ippl::Comm-&gt;size()
                &lt;&lt; ippl::endl;

    ippl::finalize();
    return 0;
}
</textarea>
    <pre id="ippl-output">Start the local runner, paste the token, then compile and run.</pre>
  </div>
  <div class="ippl-playground-status" id="ippl-status">Runner URL: http://127.0.0.1:5050</div>
</div>

<script>
(() => {
  const runnerUrl = "http://127.0.0.1:5050";
  const source = document.getElementById("ippl-source");
  const output = document.getElementById("ippl-output");
  const status = document.getElementById("ippl-status");
  const token = document.getElementById("ippl-runner-token");
  const ranks = document.getElementById("ippl-runner-ranks");
  const checkButton = document.getElementById("ippl-check-runner");
  const runButton = document.getElementById("ippl-run-code");

  function setOutput(text) {
    output.textContent = text;
  }

  function commandLine(stage) {
    if (!stage || !stage.command) return "";
    return "$ " + stage.command.join(" ") + "\nexit " + stage.returncode + "\n";
  }

  function stageText(label, stage) {
    if (!stage) return "";
    const stdout = stage.stdout ? "\nstdout:\n" + stage.stdout : "";
    const stderr = stage.stderr ? "\nstderr:\n" + stage.stderr : "";
    return "== " + label + " ==\n" + commandLine(stage) + stdout + stderr + "\n";
  }

  function formatResult(result) {
    if (!result.ok && result.error) {
      return "Error: " + result.error;
    }
    return [
      "ok: " + result.ok,
      "stage: " + result.stage,
      "ranks: " + (result.ranks || ""),
      "",
      stageText("configure", result.configure),
      stageText("build", result.build),
      stageText("run", result.run)
    ].join("\n");
  }

  async function checkRunner() {
    status.textContent = "Checking runner...";
    try {
      const response = await fetch(runnerUrl + "/health");
      const data = await response.json();
      status.textContent = data.ok
        ? "Runner is available. Paste the token printed by Docker before running code."
        : "Runner responded, but did not report ok.";
    } catch (error) {
      status.textContent = "Runner is not reachable. Start Docker with the command shown above.";
    }
  }

  async function runCode() {
    runButton.disabled = true;
    status.textContent = "Compiling and running...";
    setOutput("Submitting code to local IPPL runner...");
    try {
      const response = await fetch(runnerUrl + "/compile-run", {
        method: "POST",
        headers: {"Content-Type": "application/json"},
        body: JSON.stringify({
          token: token.value,
          source: source.value,
          ranks: Number(ranks.value || 2)
        })
      });
      const result = await response.json();
      setOutput(formatResult(result));
      status.textContent = result.ok ? "Run completed." : "Run failed at stage: " + (result.stage || "request");
    } catch (error) {
      setOutput(String(error));
      status.textContent = "Could not contact local runner.";
    } finally {
      runButton.disabled = false;
    }
  }

  checkButton.addEventListener("click", checkRunner);
  runButton.addEventListener("click", runCode);
  checkRunner();
})();
</script>
```

This first playground intentionally avoids arbitrary project structure, external files, and long-running jobs. The local runner accepts one source file, clamps the MPI rank count, applies compile/run timeouts, and returns stdout and stderr.
